claim_scope.surface: current Python Wardline-Core plus Wardline-Governance recertification, with Wardline-Type explicitly out of scope

id_schema.G-LITE-<slug>: Lite governance profile obligation (only valid in Lite ledgers, not this one)
id_schema.P1-S0-EXAMPLE-<slug> / C-CRIT-0-EXAMPLE-: Reserved illustrative namespace (§15.1); MUST NOT be used in real ledgers

obligations.summary: Precision and recall are measured, tracked, and published per cell.

claim_scope.applies_to: per-cell precision and recall measurement and publication

evidence_classes.note: Live conformance run records 17 failing cells; non-compliance is honestly reflected in this ledger entry.

obligations.notes: Live report records 17 failing cells and the floor semantics drift across prose and verifier behavior.

obligations.summary: Golden corpus exists with coherent specimen schema and adversarial coverage.

claim_scope.applies_to: golden corpus schema and adversarial coverage

evidence_classes.note: Adversarial coverage minima per §15.2(6) and §11 must be demonstrated for every claimed framework rule.

obligations.notes: Live schema, verdict vocabulary, and known_false_negative handling drift from §11.

obligations.summary: Enforcement tools pass their own rules where applicable.

claim_scope.applies_to: self-hosting gate for the Python reference scanner

evidence_classes.note: The conformance report is produced by running the scanner on its own source (self-hosting). The report is in conformance JSON format, not raw SARIF; the evidence class is conformance_report (the output format) rather than self_hosting_sarif (which would denote a SARIF-format output file). The self_hosting_input_hash in freshness_binding binds this evidence to the specific scanner inputs that were analysed.

obligations.notes: Current gate only blocks on unexcepted error findings; the full self-hosting gate definition is not yet satisfied. Evidence class corrected from self_hosting_sarif to conformance_report because wardline.conformance.json is a conformance summary, not a raw SARIF file.

obligations.summary: Verification-mode SARIF is deterministic and assessor-runnable.

claim_scope.applies_to: deterministic SARIF v2.1.0 output in verification mode

evidence_classes.note: Covers verification-mode determinism and result/annotation-group ordering via test_results_sorted_by_file_and_line, test_annotation_groups_sorted, test_degradations_are_sorted, and test_deterministic_true_in_verification_mode.

obligations.notes: Result ordering is deterministic under the current test suite. End-to-end re-verification against unsorted file discovery remains outstanding; this gap is honestly recorded here rather than claimed as runnable SARIF evidence against the emitter source file.

obligations.summary: Assurance governance minimums — protected-file review, temporal separation, exception tracking, and annotation change tracking — are present and reviewable at the level required by §15.3.2 Assurance.

claim_scope.applies_to: Assurance-level governance minimums

evidence_classes.note: CODEOWNERS protects governance artefacts; a concrete ratification artifact is still missing.

evidence_classes.target: git history for wardline.yaml, .github/CODEOWNERS, and wardline.exceptions.json
evidence_classes.note: Temporal-separation audit should walk the git history for the governance artifacts wardline.yaml, .github/CODEOWNERS, and wardline.exceptions.json. The audit subject is now concrete, but a bound review artifact is still missing.

obligations.notes: Manifest, CODEOWNERS, and exception register exist; a bound assessment run covering protected-file review, temporal separation, exception integrity, and annotation-change review does not yet exist; therefore implemented_no_evidence is the honest state.

obligations.summary: The Wardline manifest system is consumed by the tools that depend on it, and JSON Schema validation is performed either by that tool or by a declared Wardline-Governance tool in the same regime.

claim_scope.applies_to: manifest consumption and schema validation enforcement

evidence_classes.note: Schema validation is performed against the root manifest; evidence artifact not yet bound.
Coherence check runs against the manifest; implementation is in src/wardline/manifest/coherence.py but evidence artifact not yet bound.

obligations.notes: Implementation exists in src/wardline/manifest/ (loader, schemas, coherence) and is exercised by CLI subcommands. Evidence targets now name the manifest being validated rather than the implementation modules. A bound validation-output artifact is missing; therefore implemented_no_evidence is the honest state.

obligations.summary: Taint propagation preserves the normative join algebra with MIXED_RAW absorbing.

claim_scope.applies_to: taint join algebra in callgraph propagation

evidence_classes.note: Seven cross-classification combination tests verify taint_join() algebra at the L3 propagation level: cross-family merges produce MIXED_RAW, UNKNOWN-family demotion preserved, single-callee identity, MIXED_RAW absorption, GUARDED+EXTERNAL_RAW coverage.
Hypothesis property test verifying propagation result is independent of worklist processing order via name permutation (200 examples).

obligations.notes: Fixed: L3 callgraph propagation now uses taint_join() for callee combination per §6 join algebra. TRUST_RANK retained for floor clamps (ordering comparison, not taint combination). Cross-classification merges correctly produce MIXED_RAW. SCC pre-initialization ensures order-independent results for recursive graphs with cross-classification L1 taints. State evidenced pending BAR pipeline review.

obligations.summary: Corpus release, integrity, and review model support independent assessment.

claim_scope.applies_to: golden corpus release integrity and independent review model

obligations.notes: State preserved as implemented_no_evidence: hash manifest exists and corpus_verify is runnable, but separate publication and review-model evidence for independent assessment are incomplete. corpus_hash bound into freshness_binding because the evidence class is corpus_verify.

obligations.summary: Analysis level 1 truthfully represents the minimum conformant scope.

claim_scope.applies_to: Python binding analysis-level 1 minimum-conformance scope

evidence_classes.note: Review confirms analysis-level gating: general callgraph taint propagation runs only at L3, so L1 cannot deliver the two-hop scope promised by §15.2(4) and the binding's §A.3.
AST inspection of the callgraph propagation pass shows it is only invoked from the L3 code path, contradicting the binding's L1 minimum-scope claim.
Existing unit tests for callgraph taint propagation exercise only the L3 entry point; no L1-level test demonstrates the required two-hop scope.

obligations.notes: The binding promises required two-hop scope at level 1, but general callgraph taint propagation only runs at level 3. State remains non_compliant; this is compliance debt that MUST NOT be papered over.

obligations.summary: The Lite governance checklist remains explicit and assessor-runnable.

claim_scope.applies_to: Lite governance checklist (out of scope for the Assurance-only BAR ledger)

evidence_classes.note: The compliance-ledger schema is specialized for §15.3.2 Assurance with §15.3.4 Bootstrap Assurance Reference (schema description explicitly states 'Lite deployments MUST use a separate schema'). A Lite-profile obligation therefore cannot appear as a live obligation in this ledger; the not_applicable state is a structural consequence of the schema's declared scope, independent of what wardline.yaml currently declares. The Assurance-equivalent substance is covered by the G-ASSURANCE-* obligations in this same ledger.

obligations.notes: Marked not_applicable under the Assurance-only BAR ledger schema. The obligation id is preserved per §15.1's stable-id rule. The substance (explicit, assessor-runnable governance minimums) is covered under Assurance by the following new obligations in this ledger: G-ASSURANCE-CHECKLIST-VERIFIABLE, G-ASSURANCE-TEMPORAL-SEPARATION, G-ASSURANCE-COHERENCE-GATE, G-ASSURANCE-ADVERSARIAL-CORPUS-MINIMA, G-ASSURANCE-FINGERPRINT-BASELINE, G-ASSURANCE-EXPEDITED-RATIO, and G-ASSURANCE-SIEM-EXPORT.

obligations.summary: Release sign-off requires a normal-law SARIF run; sign-off MUST NOT be taken from alternate-law or direct-law output.

claim_scope.applies_to: release sign-off control-law gate

evidence_classes.note: Release-signoff SARIF artifact must show `wardline.controlLaw: "normal"` for the bound snapshot. Artifact not yet bound; state remains `implemented_no_evidence` until captured.
Unit tests for the three-state control-law model (normal/alternate/direct) emitted to SARIF run properties.

obligations.notes: Three-state control-law model and SARIF emission are implemented, but no current release-signoff scan run has been bound to the ledger evidence artefact. State remains implemented_no_evidence pending a bound normal-law run.

obligations.summary: Any alternate-law or direct-law window is closed by a verifiable retrospective scan (wardline.retroactiveScan=true with matching range) before release sign-off.

claim_scope.applies_to: degraded-law window closure via retrospective scan before release sign-off

evidence_classes.note: The first returning normal-law SARIF artifact must show `wardline.retroactiveScan: true` and the matching retrospective range. Artifact not yet bound; state remains `implemented_no_evidence`.
Unit tests for the retrospective-scan SARIF properties at both result and run level.

obligations.notes: Retrospective-scan SARIF semantics and the scan --retrospective CLI flag are implemented, but the current release evidence does not yet bind any recent history to a confirmed closure check. State remains implemented_no_evidence pending that binding.

obligations.summary: Regime documentation covers the narrowed claimed surface with no unexplained gaps.

claim_scope.applies_to: regime coverage documentation for the claimed surface

evidence_classes.note: Cross-reference the regime documentation's declared surface against the obligation catalog. The top-level catalog_status is 'partial' and R-CATALOG-COMPLETENESS is non_compliant, so this check surfaces at least one known gap. The machine ledger is the primary artifact; the human-readable doc is secondary.

obligations.notes: State non_compliant: §15.4 requires coverage completeness and the top-level catalog_status is 'partial'. The regime cannot honestly claim 'no unexplained gaps' while R-CATALOG-COMPLETENESS itself is non_compliant. Evidence now targets the machine ledger; the human-readable verification doc is secondary and currently drifted.

obligations.summary: §15.6 worked examples use only placeholder obligation identifiers drawn from the §15.1 reserved illustrative namespace and carry explicit framing text declaring them illustrative and non-factual.

claim_scope.applies_to: §15.6 worked-example credibility under the §15.1 reserved illustrative namespace

evidence_classes.note: Captured check output proving that all IDs used in §15.6.1 and §15.6.2 are reserved-namespace placeholders and that none leak into the live ledger.

obligations.notes: Reformed obligation under the §15.6 spec redesign. The spec now uses reserved illustrative-namespace placeholder IDs and carries explicit 'illustrative, non-factual' framing in §15.6.1 and §15.6.2. The reformed test is described in the evidence_classes entry but has NOT been bound to a specific run yet. Honest state is implemented_no_evidence: the implementation (the spec redesign) exists, assessor-runnable evidence (a captured run of the 3-part check) is pending. The first BAR pipeline run over this obligation will transition it to evidenced.

obligations.summary: The obligation catalog is complete for the claimed surface.

claim_scope.applies_to: obligation catalog completeness for the claimed surface

evidence_classes.note: Diff the required-surface enumeration in catalog_sources against the obligations array. A complete catalog has no gaps; catalog_status is currently 'partial', so this check surfaces the known gaps. The machine ledger is the primary artifact.

obligations.notes: State remains non_compliant per the honest-evidence principle: the ledger currently has 25 obligations, but the full Part I and Part II-A obligation surface (every rule, every criterion, every governance MUST) is not yet fully enumerated. The top-level catalog_status is 'partial' reflecting this. Evidence now targets the machine ledger and catalog_sources rather than the drifted human-readable doc.

obligations.summary: The derived release projection is assessor-runnable and declares scope, state, next action, reviewer status, and explicit not_applicable rows.

claim_scope.applies_to: release projection runnability and row coverage

evidence_classes.note: Cross-check certification matrix rows against the §15.1 and §15.6 release-gate column requirements: row identifier, claimed scope, backing obligations, current state, next action, reviewer status, and explicit not_applicable rows.

obligations.notes: State non_compliant: the certification matrix is not assessor-runnable. Current defects: (1) rows C02, C03, and C08 use open-text scopes instead of explicit backing obligation IDs; (2) row C04 uses 'at risk' which is not derivable from the matrix's own working rules that non-verified/non-not_applicable backing obligations block release; (3) row language prevents assessor-runnable projection logic. The matrix is implementation_surface and evidence_classes only; the normative source refs are §15.1 and §15.6.

obligations.summary: Derived views (human-readable certification matrix, compliance ledger doc) are synchronized with the machine-readable compliance ledger and do not illegally claim source-of-truth status.

claim_scope.applies_to: derived view synchronization and source-of-truth discipline

evidence_classes.note: Cross-check the human-readable docs against wardline.compliance.json. §15.1 defines the machine ledger as the source of truth; derived views must not claim primary status or contain stale data.

obligations.notes: State non_compliant: the human-readable compliance ledger and certification matrix are currently stale and illegally claim source-of-truth status. Per §15.1, these are derived views; wardline.compliance.json is the primary artifact. The docs must be regenerated or marked as secondary views before this obligation can be satisfied.

obligations.summary: The Assurance governance checklist enumerated in §15.3.2 is explicit, assessor-runnable, and distinguishable from the Lite checklist.

claim_scope.applies_to: Assurance governance checklist explicit enumeration

evidence_classes.note: Captured review of §15.3.2 confirming the Assurance checklist is explicit, assessor-runnable, and distinct from the Lite checklist.

obligations.notes: Meta-obligation asserting that §15.3.2 documents the Assurance requirements table distinctly from the Lite checklist. The table exists in the spec at §15.3.2 but no bound static_code_review run has been captured, so state is implemented_no_evidence, not evidenced. Follows the same precedent as G-ASSURANCE-COHERENCE-GATE (code exists, binding evidence absent).

obligations.summary: Temporal separation between policy changes and code changes is operational with no alternatives under the Assurance profile.

claim_scope.applies_to: Assurance temporal separation, no alternatives

evidence_classes.note: Declaration check: wardline.yaml declares temporal_separation.alternative="enforced" and the manifest schema validates this. Under §15.3.4 BAR, single-actor workflows satisfy temporal separation via commit ordering; the declaration confirms the enforcement posture. A full commit-history audit walk is a separate piece of work that will upgrade the evidence from declaration-level to history-level.

obligations.notes: Mechanism is authorised by §15.3.4 (single-actor commit ordering satisfies temporal separation under BAR). wardline.yaml declares temporal_separation.alternative="enforced". Source_ref corrected from §10.1 (exceptionability classes) to §10.2 (governance mechanisms, where temporal separation is normatively defined). Evidence is a declaration check against the manifest; a bound commit-history audit walk is pending.

obligations.summary: Manifest coherence is enforced as a blocking gate before code-level enforcement under the Assurance profile.

claim_scope.applies_to: Assurance coherence-gate blocking before enforcement

evidence_classes.note: Coherence check implementation exists. The governance binding that makes it release-blocking (CI gate on sign-off) is not yet wired, so state is implemented_no_evidence rather than evidenced.

obligations.notes: Coherence module exists in src/wardline/manifest/coherence.py; a CI binding that makes it a blocking release gate is pending. State implemented_no_evidence: code exists, gate-binding evidence absent.

obligations.summary: The golden corpus meets the Assurance-level adversarial minima: at least one adversarial_false_positive and one adversarial_false_negative per claimed framework rule, plus any binding-defined suppression_interaction minima.

claim_scope.applies_to: Assurance adversarial corpus minima coverage

evidence_classes.note: Assurance uplift over C-CRIT-6. The live corpus does not meet the full §15.6 Step 3 adversarial minima across all claimed framework rules; state is non_compliant and bound to C-CRIT-6's resolution.

obligations.notes: Assurance-specific uplift on the C-CRIT-6 corpus obligation. Honestly non_compliant: the live corpus has partial adversarial coverage but does not meet the per-rule minima §15.6 requires for Assurance claims.

obligations.summary: A structured annotation fingerprint baseline with canonical hashing exists, covering the claimed annotation surface with three-category change detection (added, modified, removed).

claim_scope.applies_to: Assurance fingerprint baseline with canonical hashing

evidence_classes.note: Fingerprint baseline exists at wardline.fingerprint.json, generated 2026-04-11T18:47:58. wardline regime verify reports fingerprint_baseline_exists=PASS and fingerprint_baseline_fresh=PASS (age 1 day < interval 180 days). The baseline covers the annotation surface with the canonical hashing scheme. Review under BAR pending independence is outstanding.

obligations.notes: State changed from non_compliant to evidenced: wardline.fingerprint.json exists, is schema-valid, and regime verify passes both baseline-exists and baseline-fresh checks. The prior non_compliant state was based on an incorrect claim that no baseline artefact existed — the file was present but missed during the initial migration. Honest correction.

obligations.summary: The expedited governance ratio is computed, a threshold is declared in the root manifest, and automated governance-level findings fire when the threshold is exceeded.

claim_scope.applies_to: Assurance expedited governance ratio with declared threshold and automated findings

evidence_classes.note: Captured `wardline regime verify --manifest wardline.yaml --path . --json` output for the bound snapshot must show `expedited_ratio_threshold_declared` passing and `expedited_ratio` evaluated against the declared threshold.

obligations.notes: The root manifest declares `metadata.expedited_ratio_threshold: 0.15`, and the regime verifier computes the expedited ratio against that threshold. Independent review is still pending, so the honest state is `evidenced`, not `verified`.

obligations.summary: Structured governance events are exported to a SIEM for security monitoring. SHOULD generally; MUST for formally accredited systems.

claim_scope.applies_to: Assurance SIEM export of governance events

evidence_classes.note: §15.3.2 marks SIEM export as SHOULD (MUST only for formally accredited systems). Under §15.3.4 BAR, the reference implementation MUST NOT claim formal accreditation while BAR is active (mutual-exclusion clause), so the MUST does not apply. The SHOULD is advisory and does not create a binding requirement under the declared profile. The not_applicable state captures 'the MUST is unreachable under BAR and the SHOULD is not a binding requirement in this context'. Reviewer attestation cites the BAR ADR's mutual-exclusion clause.

obligations.notes: State changed from implemented_no_evidence to not_applicable. The SHOULD (non-binding) + BAR mutual-exclusion with accreditation together make this obligation out of scope for the current declared profile. When the project graduates out of BAR and seeks formal accreditation, this obligation will transition to non_compliant/implemented_no_evidence/evidenced as the SIEM export is wired and exercised. Until then, not_applicable is the honest state.

obligations.summary: The manifest schema enforces BAR-specific conditional constraints, including requiring graduation_auditor for external_audit and forbidding same-actor temporal-separation alternatives under Assurance/BAR.

claim_scope.applies_to: BAR manifest schema enforcement

evidence_classes.note: Schema enforces: (1) governance_profile is required at top level (no silent defaults); (2) when BAR is declared (assurance + bootstrap_assurance_reference), same-actor temporal-separation is forbidden; (3) graduation_mechanism=external_audit requires graduation_auditor. BAR is optional per §15.3.4 MAY. Negative probes confirm all invalid variants are rejected.

obligations.notes: Fixed: wardline.schema.json enforces BAR conditionals correctly. governance_profile is required (no silent defaults). BAR (bootstrap_assurance_reference) is optional per §15.3.4 MAY, but when declared under assurance, same-actor temporal-separation is forbidden. Loader no longer silently defaults governance_profile. State evidenced pending BAR pipeline review.
